description_long: This object resembles a large, rounded burlap sack with a tightly cinched top, secured by a white band. The main body is textured with a woven, brown pattern, giving it the appearance of a rough, fibrous material. Around the base, a dark, metallic-looking band encircles the sack, suggesting reinforcement or containment. On one side, a hexagonal green gem or panel is embedded, possibly serving as an indicator or activation interface. Another side features a small, circular white component, which could be a trigger or fuse mechanism. The overall design is utilitarian, with a mix of rustic and modern elements, making it stand out in an urban environment.
description: A large, burlap-textured bomb with a cinched top, metallic base, and a green hexagonal panel on one side. It features a white band and a small circular component, blending rustic and modern design cues.
description_view_0: The side view shows the brown, woven texture and the cinched top, with a white circular component visible on the right.
description_view_1: The rear view highlights the full, rounded body and the cinched top, with no visible panels or components.
description_view_2: This side view mirrors view 0 but without the white circular component, showing only the woven texture and cinched top.
description_view_3: The front view displays the green hexagonal panel prominently, set into the woven body, with the cinched top and metallic base visible.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: The bomb appears to have a green hexagonal panel that may function as an activation or control interface, and a white circular component that could serve as a trigger or fuse.
